symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced level of consciousness / floppy, unrousable neonate

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia with salt-wasting crisis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuroglycopenia/hypoperfusion from cortisol deficiency, hypoglycaemia and shock.
Altered consciousness in a neonate is a cardinal sepsis red flag.

symptoms.duration: worsening over 24 hours; no feeds for 8 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia with salt-wasting crisis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting drives salt and volume loss in a salt-wasting crisis.
Vomiting is compatible but the age and shock argue strongly against pyloric stenosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe dehydration signs — sunken fontanelle, absent tears

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia with salt-wasting crisis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Renal salt wasting plus vomiting produces marked volume depletion; carer-observable.
Peripheral shutdown is a hallmark of uncompensated neonatal shock; visible to a carer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Genital atypia noted at birth (clitoromegaly / partial labial fusion), specialist review pending

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia with salt-wasting crisis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Virilisation of a 46,XX infant from adrenal hyperandrogenism is the anatomical signature of CAH; combined with a salt-wasting collapse it is near-specific.
Neonates are frequently afebrile or hypothermic when septic; absence of fever does NOT exclude sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound hypotension and tachycardia (BP ~60/35, HR ~185) with weak central pulses and delayed capillary refill

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective uncompensated shock — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers circulatory compromise from carer-visible mottling and reduced responsiveness, never from a measured BP.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated 17-OHP; hyponatraemia; hyperkalaemia; often hypoglycaemia — anticipated, pending hospital bloods
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis unconfirmed and steroid/electrolyte correction undirected — but this is a hospital investigation, never obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia with salt-wasting crisis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Markedly elevated 17-OHP with the salt-wasting electrolyte pattern is confirmatory — but sealed and only available in hospital.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Congenital adrenal hyperplasia with salt-wasting crisis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A vomiting, dehydrated, shocked neonate in the second week of life with genital atypia noted at birth is the classic salt-wasting-crisis presentation and demands immediate emergency transfer.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital bloods — electrolytes, glucose, 17-hydroxyprogesterone, cortisol, blood gas, cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct the diagnosis and guide fluid/steroid/electrolyte correction — but these are performed on arrival in hospital, not requested via telehealth. The AI's action is escalation, not investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Any attempt to obtain venous bloods or IV access at home
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not achievable or appropriate for a carer or the telehealth AI; must not delay the 000 call.
